{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Marketing Executive",
  "description": "Castel Resources Consultancy Limited unlocks your business&#39;s full potential with our comprehensive HR outsourcing services. Our expert team handles everything from recruitment and payroll processing to employee benefits administration and compliance, freeing you from administrative burdens and ensuring legal compliance. With advanced HR technology, strategic planning, and dedicated support for employee development and relations, we streamline your HR functions, allowing you to focus on your core business activities and drive growth. Partner with us to elevate your HR capabilities and achieve seamless, efficient operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Plan and implement marketing campaigns
	Promote products and services to potential customers
	Conduct market research to identify new opportunities
	Manage social media and digital marketing platforms
	Support sales team in generating leads
	Organize promotional activities and events
	Monitor marketing performance and prepare reports.


Requirements


	HND / BSc in Marketing, Business Administration, or related field
	2&ndash;3 years of experience in marketing or sales
	Good communication and negotiation skills
	Basic knowledge of digital marketing and social media
	Creative, proactive, and results-driven.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;castelhrjobs@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Marketing Executive Job at Castel Resources Consultancy Limited




        
    

        
        Castel Resources Consultancy Limited unlocks your business's full potential with our comprehensive HR outsourcing services. Our expert team handles everything from recruitment and payroll processing to employee benefits administration and compliance, freeing you from administrative burdens and ensuring legal compliance. With advanced HR technology, strategic planning, and dedicated support for employee development and relations, we streamline your HR functions, allowing you to focus on your core business activities and drive growth. Partner with us to elevate your HR capabilities and achieve seamless, efficient operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Executive
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Plan and implement marketing campaigns
	Promote products and services to potential customers
	Conduct market research to identify new opportunities
	Manage social media and digital marketing platforms
	Support sales team in generating leads
	Organize promotional activities and events
	Monitor marketing performance and prepare reports.


Requirements


	HND / BSc in Marketing, Business Administration, or related field
	2–3 years of experience in marketing or sales
	Good communication and negotiation skills
	Basic knowledge of digital marketing and social media
	Creative, proactive, and results-driven.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Analyst",
  "description": "Brooks Advisory Services Limited (BASL) is a Management Consulting firm dedicated to providing Human Resource services to SMEs across Africa.We are committed to embedding ourselves in our clients&#39; businesses to deliver essential HR solutions that foster an efficient and effective work environment. Our team consists of experienced mid-level HR professionals equipped with the expertise to implement our solutions effectively, supporting business growth across various industries.

Our clients focus on Business Development, while Brooks Advisory Services Limited (BASL) handles Internal Development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Analyst

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are recruiting a Human Resource Analyst for a restaurant in Abuja.
	The successful candidate will work on-site as the dedicated HR personnel for the restaurant, while working with Brooks Advisory Services Limited (BASL) to implement structured HR processes, policies, and performance systems.
	This role is suited for an HR professional who is detail-oriented, operationally sound, and data-driven, with a strong understanding of people operations within a hospitality or service-based environment.


Key Responsibilities
HR Operations &amp; Data Management:


	Maintain accurate employee records, contracts, and HR documentation
	Track staff attendance, shifts, leave, and overtime records
	Compile and analyses HR data related to staffing, performance, and turnover.


Recruitment &amp; Onboarding Support:


	Support recruitment activities for restaurant staff roles
	Coordinate interviews, documentation, and onboarding processes
	Ensure smooth induction of new employees in line with BASL standards.


Performance Tracking &amp; Reporting:


	Support the implementation of performance appraisal systems
	Monitor KPIs, productivity, and staff efficiency
	Prepare periodic HR reports for management and BASL.


Employee Relations &amp; Compliance:


	Serve as a point of contact for employee HR-related inquiries
	Support disciplinary processes and documentation
	Ensure compliance with labour laws and internal HR policies.


Payroll &amp; Compensation Support:


	Validate payroll inputs, including attendance, leave, and deductions
	Support timely salary processing in collaboration with accounts
	Maintain accurate records of compensation and benefits.


Training &amp; Development Support:


	Coordinate staff training schedules and learning records
	Track training needs and participation
	Support skills development initiatives across departments.


Policy &amp; Process Implementation:


	Implement HR policies, SOPs, and guidelines provided by BASL
	Ensure consistent application across all restaurant staff
	Support HR audits and process improvement initiatives.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field
	2 - 4 years of HR experience, preferably in hospitality, food service, or service-driven organizations
	Strong administrative, analytical, and reporting skills
	Good knowledge of Nigerian labour laws
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and HR reporting tools
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
	Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
	Ability to work on-site in a fast-paced environment.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;jobs@brooksadvisoryservices.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-01",

  },

Human Resource Analyst Job at Brooks Advisory Services Limited




        
    

        
        Brooks Advisory Services Limited (BASL) is a Management Consulting firm dedicated to providing Human Resource services to SMEs across Africa.We are committed to embedding ourselves in our clients' businesses to deliver essential HR solutions that foster an efficient and effective work environment. Our team consists of experienced mid-level HR professionals equipped with the expertise to implement our solutions effectively, supporting business growth across various industries.

Our clients focus on Business Development, while Brooks Advisory Services Limited (BASL) handles Internal Development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Analyst
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are recruiting a Human Resource Analyst for a restaurant in Abuja.
	The successful candidate will work on-site as the dedicated HR personnel for the restaurant, while working with Brooks Advisory Services Limited (BASL) to implement structured HR processes, policies, and performance systems.
	This role is suited for an HR professional who is detail-oriented, operationally sound, and data-driven, with a strong understanding of people operations within a hospitality or service-based environment.


Key Responsibilities
HR Operations & Data Management:


	Maintain accurate employee records, contracts, and HR documentation
	Track staff attendance, shifts, leave, and overtime records
	Compile and analyses HR data related to staffing, performance, and turnover.


Recruitment & Onboarding Support:


	Support recruitment activities for restaurant staff roles
	Coordinate interviews, documentation, and onboarding processes
	Ensure smooth induction of new employees in line with BASL standards.


Performance Tracking & Reporting:


	Support the implementation of performance appraisal systems
	Monitor KPIs, productivity, and staff efficiency
	Prepare periodic HR reports for management and BASL.


Employee Relations & Compliance:


	Serve as a point of contact for employee HR-related inquiries
	Support disciplinary processes and documentation
	Ensure compliance with labour laws and internal HR policies.


Payroll & Compensation Support:


	Validate payroll inputs, including attendance, leave, and deductions
	Support timely salary processing in collaboration with accounts
	Maintain accurate records of compensation and benefits.


Training & Development Support:


	Coordinate staff training schedules and learning records
	Track training needs and participation
	Support skills development initiatives across departments.


Policy & Process Implementation:


	Implement HR policies, SOPs, and guidelines provided by BASL
	Ensure consistent application across all restaurant staff
	Support HR audits and process improvement initiatives.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field
	2 - 4 years of HR experience, preferably in hospitality, food service, or service-driven organizations
	Strong administrative, analytical, and reporting skills
	Good knowledge of Nigerian labour laws
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and HR reporting tools
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
	Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
	Ability to work on-site in a fast-paced environment.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Experience Center Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Abeokuta, Ogun

About The Role


	The Sun King Experience Center Executive is task with overseeing the overall wellbeing of the experience center, inventory management and ensuring a high level of customer satisfaction.

Inventory management:

	Have the ability to gauge client needs.
	Are Familiar with MS Excel and MS Word

Experience Center Executive Job at Greenlight Planet




        
    

        
        Greenlight Planet is a multinational, for-profit business that designs, distributes, and finances solar-powered home energy products, with an underserved population in mind: the 1.3 billion global consumers for whom the old-fashioned electrical grid is either unavailable or too expensive.

Over a decade in business, the company is now a leading global brand in emerging markets across Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa.  Greenlight’s Sun King™ products provide modern light and energy to 26 million people in more than 40 countries and have sold over 10.5 million products worldwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Experience Center Executive
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Abeokuta, Ogun

About The Role


	The Sun King Experience Center Executive is task with overseeing the overall wellbeing of the experience center, inventory management and ensuring a high level of customer satisfaction.
	This role also assist the Sun King Experience Center Supervisor in generating sales prospect and converting them into actual sales. This role reports to the Solar Inverter Manager.
	Department EasyBuy Direct Sales Employment Type Permanent - Full Time Location Nigeria Workplace type Onsite Reporting To Solar Inverter Manager


What you would be expected to do
Inventory management:


	Coordinate with warehouse to receive stock and keep track of inventory
	Issue out stocks to cash sales customers as per requirement
	Maintain an updated Goods Received and Goods Issued log online
	Perform daily cycle counts and monthly stock take
	Scan in and out Sun King inventory on a serial basis
	Communicate any inventory shortage, theft, or loss at the zonal warehouse


Reports:


	Prepare daily on hand stock report for stakeholders use
	Report on daily issued out stock for replacement and or replenishment


Repairs:


	Repairs and Spare Parts management
	Receive faulty components, identify the technical issue and verify warranty eligibility through several technical checks
	Perform basic check on the faulty units before replacement


Store management:


	Ensure the center is well maintained tidy and that the store arrangement is per SKU.
	Ensure neatness and good ambiance of the experience center at all times.
	Observation of safety procedures to avert hazards at the experience center.
	Welcome walk-in customers and provide assistance as necessary.


Requirements
You might be a strong candidate if you:


	Have a Degree or its equivalent from a recognized institution in any related field.
	Have at least 1 years of hands on experience in a related role.
	Have effective verbal, communication and people skills.
	Are able to demonstrate high levels of integrity with a proactive and positive attitude.
	Have a good customer centric attitude and focus.
	Have a team player spirit with high levels of flexibility.
	Possess good problem-solving skills.
	Have the ability to gauge client needs.
	Are Familiar with MS Excel and MS Word
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Controller",
  "description": "NutriK KFZE, a proud subsidiary of the globally recognized Nutriset Group, plays a vital role in producing and distributing high?quality ready?to?use foods that support the treatment and prevention of malnutrition across Nigeria. Our mission is driven by purpose: to help save lives and improve the well?being of children and families in need. We specialize in the production of essential nutritional products including Ready?to?Use Therapeutic Food (RUTF), Ready?to?Use Supplementary Food (RUSF), and Lipid?Based Nutrient Supplements (LNS) which support humanitarian and health interventions nationwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Controller
Job Location:&nbsp;Kano
Slots: 2

Overall Mission
General objective:


	To ensure consistent product quality and safety by conducting thorough inspections, sensory evaluations, and internal controls across all stages of production.
	The role aims to prevent non?conformities, support continuous improvement, maintain accurate documentation, and facilitate smooth shift transitions ultimately guaranteeing that only compliant, high?quality products are released&nbsp;


Responsibilities and Tasks


	Serve as a panelist for the sensory evaluation of raw materials, packaging materials, and finished products.
	Assess and verify the conformity of transport vehicles, and promptly notify the appropriate team when deviations are detected.
	Collect samples for every reception and confirm the availability of Certificates of Analysis (COA).
	Conduct a thorough handover at the end of each shift, highlighting any issues, non?conformities, or challenges encountered.
	Participate in root cause analysis (RCA) for quality defects identified during production.
	Update the non?conformities follow?up sheet with all defects observed during the shift.
	Collect finished product samples from each production line for daily sensory evaluation.
	Maintain and update the defect logbook consistently.
	Adhere to and execute the internal control plan for production equipment.
	Authorize the restart of production lines after any stoppage, cleaning, or maintenance activity occurring during the shift.
	Ensure the availability of essential production consumables including 70&deg; alcohol, paper towels, masks, nurse caps, and required PPE and verify that all containers are properly labeled.
	Report any incidents observed while performing assigned duties.
	Carry out any additional relevant tasks assigned by the line manager.


Required Skills:


	Language skills &ndash; English &amp; Hausa is essential.
	Education degree &ndash; Diploma in Food Science or any other relevant discipline.
	Work experience; 2Years in food industry years&rsquo; experience in same position or related field
	Computer skills &ndash; MS Word, MS Excel &amp; Outlook


Other required skills:


	Ability to create and follow schedules.
	Leadership and interpersonal skills
	Problem solving skills.
	Communication skills
	Reporting Skills
	Team working skills.
	Confidence.


Note


	Only candidates who meet the selection criteria will be contacted.
	This position is open to Nigerian nationals only.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-11",

    "@type": "Organization",

Quality Controller Job at NutriK KFZE




        
    

        
        NutriK KFZE, a proud subsidiary of the globally recognized Nutriset Group, plays a vital role in producing and distributing high?quality ready?to?use foods that support the treatment and prevention of malnutrition across Nigeria. Our mission is driven by purpose: to help save lives and improve the well?being of children and families in need. We specialize in the production of essential nutritional products including Ready?to?Use Therapeutic Food (RUTF), Ready?to?Use Supplementary Food (RUSF), and Lipid?Based Nutrient Supplements (LNS) which support humanitarian and health interventions nationwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Controller
Job Location: Kano
Slots: 2
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Overall Mission
General objective:


	To ensure consistent product quality and safety by conducting thorough inspections, sensory evaluations, and internal controls across all stages of production.
	The role aims to prevent non?conformities, support continuous improvement, maintain accurate documentation, and facilitate smooth shift transitions ultimately guaranteeing that only compliant, high?quality products are released 


Responsibilities and Tasks


	Serve as a panelist for the sensory evaluation of raw materials, packaging materials, and finished products.
	Assess and verify the conformity of transport vehicles, and promptly notify the appropriate team when deviations are detected.
	Collect samples for every reception and confirm the availability of Certificates of Analysis (COA).
	Conduct a thorough handover at the end of each shift, highlighting any issues, non?conformities, or challenges encountered.
	Participate in root cause analysis (RCA) for quality defects identified during production.
	Update the non?conformities follow?up sheet with all defects observed during the shift.
	Collect finished product samples from each production line for daily sensory evaluation.
	Maintain and update the defect logbook consistently.
	Adhere to and execute the internal control plan for production equipment.
	Authorize the restart of production lines after any stoppage, cleaning, or maintenance activity occurring during the shift.
	Ensure the availability of essential production consumables including 70° alcohol, paper towels, masks, nurse caps, and required PPE and verify that all containers are properly labeled.
	Report any incidents observed while performing assigned duties.
	Carry out any additional relevant tasks assigned by the line manager.


Required Skills:


	Language skills – English & Hausa is essential.
	Education degree – Diploma in Food Science or any other relevant discipline.
	Work experience; 2Years in food industry years’ experience in same position or related field
	Computer skills – MS Word, MS Excel & Outlook


Other required skills:


	Ability to create and follow schedules.
	Leadership and interpersonal skills
	Problem solving skills.
	Communication skills
	Reporting Skills
	Team working skills.
	Confidence.


Note


	Only candidates who meet the selection criteria will be contacted.
	This position is open to Nigerian nationals only.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
